text: By the stars when they fade away!
Your fellow man <sup foot_note=1117>1117</sup> is neither misguided nor astray.
Nor does he speak of his own whims.
It is only a revelation sent down ňĻto himňļ.
He has been taught by one ňĻangelňļ of mighty power <sup foot_note=1118>1118</sup>
and great perfection, who once rose to ňĻhisňļ true form <sup foot_note=1119>1119</sup>

foot_notes.1119: Gabriel used to come to the Prophet in a human form. But he appeared to him ( Ô∑ļ ) twice in his angelic form: once at the beginning of the Prophet‚Äôs mission (when the angel manifested himself, filling the horizon, the Prophet ( Ô∑ļ ) lost his consciousness), and another time during the Prophet‚Äôs Night Journey to the seventh heaven to receive the order to pray directly from Allah ( see 17:1).

text: while on the highest point above the horizon,
then he approached ňĻthe Prophetňļ, coming so close
that he was only two arms-lengths away or even less. <sup foot_note=1120>1120</sup>

foot_notes.1120: lit., he was only two bow-draws away.

text: Then Allah revealed to His servant what He revealed ňĻthrough Gabrielňļ.
The ňĻProphet‚Äôsňļ heart did not doubt what he saw.
How can you ňĻO pagansňļ then dispute with him regarding what he saw?
And he certainly saw that ňĻangel descendňļ a second time
at the Lote Tree of the most extreme limit ňĻin the seventh heavenňļ‚ÄĒ
near which is the Garden of ňĻEternalňļ Residence‚ÄĒ
while the Lote Tree was overwhelmed with ňĻheavenlyňļ splendours!
The ňĻProphet‚Äôsňļ sight never wandered, nor did it overreach.
He certainly saw some of his Lord‚Äôs greatest signs. <sup foot_note=1121>1121</sup>

foot_notes.1121: The Prophet was taken to heaven to see some of Allah‚Äôs magnificent signs. He only saw what he was told to see.

text: Now, have you considered ňĻthe idols ofňļ L√Ęt and ‚Äô Uzza ,
and the third one, Man√Ęt , as well?
Do you ňĻprefer toňļ have sons while ňĻyou attributeňļ to Him daughters? <sup foot_note=1122>1122</sup>
Then this is ňĻtrulyňļ a biased distribution!

These ňĻidolsňļ are mere names that you and your forefathers have made up <sup foot_note=1123>1123</sup> ‚ÄĒa practice Allah has never authorized. They follow nothing but ňĻinheritedňļ assumptions and whatever ňĻtheirňļ souls desire, although ňĻtrueňļ guidance has already come to them from their Lord.

foot_notes.1123: Meaning, ‚ÄúYou call them gods while in fact they are not gods.‚ÄĚ

text: Or should every person ňĻsimplyňļ have whatever ňĻintercessorsňļ they desire? <sup foot_note=1124>1124</sup>

foot_notes.1124: Arab pagans took these idols as intermediaries to intercede for them on the Day of Judgment ( see 39:3)

text: In fact, to Allah ňĻaloneňļ belongs this world and the next.

ňĻImagineňļ how many ňĻnobleňļ angels are in the heavens! ňĻEvenňļ their intercession would be of no benefit whatsoever, until Allah gives permission to whoever He wills and ňĻonly for the people Heňļ approves.
Indeed, those who do not believe in the Hereafter label angels as female,
although they have no knowledge ňĻin supportňļ of this. They follow nothing but ňĻinheritedňļ assumptions. And surely assumptions can in no way replace the truth.
So turn away ňĻO Prophetňļ from whoever has shunned Our Reminder, <sup foot_note=1125>1125</sup> only seeking the ňĻfleetingňļ life of this world.
This is the extent of their knowledge. Surely your Lord knows best who has strayed from His Way and who is ňĻrightlyňļ guided.

To Allah ňĻaloneňļ belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth so that He may reward the evildoers according to what they did, and reward the good-doers with the finest reward <sup foot_note=1126>1126</sup> ‚ÄĒ

those who avoid major sins and shameful deeds, despite ňĻstumbling onňļ minor sins. Surely your Lord is infinite in forgiveness. He knew well what would become of you as He created you from the earth <sup foot_note=1127>1127</sup> and while you were ňĻstillňļ fetuses in the wombs of your mothers. <sup foot_note=1128>1128</sup> So do not ňĻfalselyňļ elevate yourselves. He knows best who is ňĻtrulyňļ righteous.
Have you seen the one who turned away ňĻfrom Islam,ňļ
and ňĻinitiallyňļ paid a little ňĻfor his salvationňļ, and then stopped?
Does he have the knowledge of the unseen so that he sees ňĻthe Hereafterňļ?
Or has he not been informed of what is in the Scripture of Moses,
and ňĻthat ofňļ Abraham, who ňĻperfectlyňļ fulfilled ňĻhis covenantňļ?
ňĻThey stateňļ that no soul burdened with sin will bear the burden of another,
and that each person will only have what they endeavoured towards,
and that ňĻthe outcome ofňļ their endeavours will be seen ňĻin their recordňļ,
then they will be fully rewarded,
and that to your Lord ňĻaloneňļ is the ultimate return ňĻof all thingsňļ.
Moreover, He is the One Who brings about joy and sadness. <sup foot_note=1130>1130</sup>
And He is the One Who gives life and causes death.
And He created the pairs‚ÄĒmales and females‚ÄĒ
from a sperm-drop when it is emitted.
And it is upon Him to bring about re-creation.
And He is the One Who enriches and impoverishes.
And He alone is the Lord of Sirius. <sup foot_note=1131>1131</sup>

foot_notes.1131: A star worshipped by some ancient pagans.

text: And He destroyed the first ňĻpeople ofňļ ‚Äô»ād,
and ňĻthenňļ Tham√Ľd, <sup foot_note=1132>1132</sup> sparing no one.
And before ňĻthat He destroyedňļ the people of Noah, who were truly far worse in wrongdoing and transgression.
And ňĻit wasňļ He ňĻWhoňļ turned the cities ňĻof Sodom and Gomorrahňļ upside down.
How overwhelming was what covered ňĻthemňļ! <sup foot_note=1133>1133</sup>

foot_notes.1133: The stones of baked clay that were rained upon them ( see 11:82-83).

text: Now, which of your Lord‚Äôs favours will you dispute?
This ňĻProphetňļ is a warner like earlier ones.
The approaching ňĻHourňļ has drawn near.
None but Allah can disclose it.
Do you find this revelation astonishing,
laughing ňĻat itňļ and not weeping ňĻin aweňļ,
while persisting in heedlessness?
Instead, prostrate to Allah and worship ňĻHim aloneňļ!
